description: An Italian dessert made from sweetened cream set with gelatin, usually served chilled and often paired with fruit sauce, caramel, or berries.
preparation_steps: Warm the cream with sugar and flavorings until the sugar dissolves.
Dissolve softened gelatin into the warm mixture.
Pour into molds or serving glasses.
Serve plain or with a sauce or fruit topping.

health_advice.benefits: Portion-controlled servings can make it a manageable dessert for occasional enjoyment.
health_advice.risks: Often high in saturated fat and sugar because of the cream and sweetening.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Serve in small portions and pair with fresh fruit rather than very sweet sauces.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional panna cotta uses gelatin, so it is usually not vegetarian. Vegetarian or vegan alternatives require different setting agents.
